Any Go 1.25.x user now `go install`s with zero toolchain download; GOTOOLCHAIN=local no longer hard-fails. Default install remains the full all-grammars binary per direction. go.sum unchanged; README/Makefile/backend.go docs updated. Verified build+install+tests under GOTOOLCHAIN=local go1.25.10 and subset `make build`.

Moved package main from cmd/bugbot/ to the module root so `go install github.com/dpoage/bugbot@latest` resolves (was failing: module found but no package at root). Clean cutover: removed cmd/, Makefile PKG=., README + backend.go updated to the bare path, CI go-version 1.26->1.25. Verified `go list .` name=main, `go install .` -> bugbot binary runs, make build -> subset, tests pass under go1.25.10.
prime now recommends a bazel-capable image when Bazel is detected (matches repro's 'bazel test //...'); prime_test updated; verified prime on the_cloud emits gcr.io/bazel-public/bazel:latest
doctor cross-checks sandbox image vs detected languages and warns on bazel+network=none; verified on the_cloud: WARNs typescript, python, and offline-bazel

prime dep-strategy note + doctor warn make the no-bazel-strategy/offline limitation explicit; repro interpret surfaces a bazel-specific environment_error summary+feedback and a precision-first bazel ecosystem entry
.ex/.exs classify as LangElixir with 'Elixir' persona; tree-sitter grammar + LSP intentionally left as follow-up per design
ChurnHeat/parseHeat drop LangOther paths; verified on the_cloud the top-5 heat files are now all code (was 3 config files), heat_files 4010->2885

finder-stage circuit breaker added; verified on the_cloud it trips in ~4s and the scan finishes in 11s (was 70s+) reporting unreliable. Discovered the cartographer has the same gap -> filed bugbot-1r9

Added ingest.PackageImportersScoped(snap, inScope); cartograph now scopes the package importer-graph parse to its target file set instead of the whole snapshot. contextFor only injects summaries that exist (=blast-radius packages on a Targeted scan), so scoping is byte-identical for injection while dropping import-parse cost from O(snapshot) to O(targets) on large repos. PackageImporters(snap) kept as the nil-scope wrapper (Sweep unchanged). Tested: scoped call omits out-of-scope importers; wrapper output identical to prior behavior. go test ./internal/ingest ./internal/funnel green.

Added bugbot scan --estimate: Funnel.EstimateScan(ctx,kind,changed) reuses the production builders (BlastRadius, scaleFinderForContext, chunkByLanguage, lensesByYield, buildUnits, EnumerateSeams, packagesSpanned + GetPackageSummaries) so the work breakdown is EXACT and cannot drift — anti-drift test asserts estimate FinderUnits == actual Sweep Stats.FinderRuns. Token/time projected from store.RunMetrics (extended with FinderRuns): avgTokens/finder-unit and tokens/sec throughput over recent finished runs, preferring same-kind+cartographer-mode sample; labeled built-in priors when no history (duration reported unknown). CLI prints scope/units/cartographer + projection + provenance and returns before any LLM call (verified by e2e smoke: sweep 9 units, targeted blast-radius 10 units incl diff-intent, exit 0, 0 spend). Tests green in funnel/store/cli; lint+vet+gofmt clean.

Added Funnel.regenSummaries: summarizes uncached packages CONCURRENTLY bounded by the funnel slot pool (slotLow, same class/bound as finders; cartographer runs before hypothesize so the pool is free) and persists EACH summary the instant it is produced via a single-row UpsertPackageSummaries — never batched. Persist uses context.WithoutCancel(ctx)+30s timeout so a summary produced just before cancellation is still saved. Optional budget gating mirrors the finder stage (stop launching + in-flight re-check on finderOverHard). Both cartograph (in-scan, with budget) and RefreshCartography (manual, no budget) now call it. Replaces the two sequential loops + end-of-pass batch upsert (the in-scan one used the cancelled ctx, so an interrupted large-repo pass previously discarded every computed summary). Tests: interrupted pass persists exactly the already-produced summary (old code: 0); full concurrent pass persists all; barrier client proves real concurrency (sequential regression deadlocks to the ctx deadline). go test ./... green (17 pkgs); vet+lint+gofmt clean.

Fixed on branch fix/ci-main-failures (f14b5e0, d249dbf). Mirrored production *_test.go scan exclude in collectGoFiles (12->6 leads, threshold kept at 10) and converted interpret.go to a tagged switch. Verified: go build/vet/test -race ./... green, golangci-lint 0 issues.

Made sandbox resource caps config-driven: added config.Sandbox.PidsLimit (yaml pids_limit, default 4096, env override, Validate>0) and threaded WithCPUs/WithMemoryMB/WithPidsLimit through sandboxRunOpts (cpus/memory_mb were silently dropped; pids had no knob and was stuck at 256). 256 pids crashed the Bazel JVM mid-analysis ('unable to create native thread'); 4096 lets it complete (proven via raw podman). Existing configs auto-inherit 4096 via Load-over-Default. Also fixed design_sandbox emit, starter template, wizard, and doctor smoke path. Tests: TestSandboxRunOpts_HonorsResourceCaps + go test ./... green.

Fixed in PR #86 (squash 015db78). validateSeverityInline (impact_sweep.go) runs the reachability/impact ladder per-finding inside runVerifyAndPersist so a T2 survivor is persisted with its validated severity + swept_at stamp; deterministic cases are zero-LLM, ambiguous cases do a single-finding adjudication when budget allows else defer to the bulk drain (swept_at NULL). SweepDrain kept only as the stranded/interrupted reconciliation path (chose option (a): inline at persist over moving the bulk pass before publish). Test: TestVerifyStream_InlineSeverityDownrank. CI green (build/vet/test, golangci-lint, CodeQL).

Fixed in PR #87 (squash 1032b32). New Funnel.ReverifySuspected + modeReverify replays open Tier-3 suspected findings through triage+verify WITHOUT the finder; findingToCandidate reconstructs from the durable finding (Reverify=true, PendingID="", Confidence=High to dodge triage step-1 drop). Survivors promote T3->T2 via UpsertFinding; refuted ones dismissed in the verify kill path (durable open row must leave the open set; UpdateStatus(dismissed) also suppresses, intended for a refuted reverify). Exposed as 'bugbot verify --suspected' (chose a dedicated path over overloading VerifyDrain, to keep oneshot drainToFixpoint from re-burning budget on just-orphaned findings). Tests: TestReverifySuspected_{PromotesSurvivor,DismissesRefuted,Empty}. CI green.

Bazel monorepo reproduction now works end-to-end, proven against ../aethon (bazel 8.4.2): 0 -> 2 Tier-1 reproductions in one `bugbot repro` run. cli/deploy.sh reproduced via a DIRECT shell invocation (new direct-run-first guidance); memory/smart_arena.h (double-free) reproduced via a TARGETED `bazel test --test_output=errors //memory/tests:smart_arena_repro_test` returning exit 3, which interpret() correctly demonstrated despite the read-only-cache warning flood. Root causes fixed: (1) interpret() empty bazel ranMarkers / no exit-3 handling; (2) read-only-cache warnings misread as env failure; (3) agent ran //... instead of targeted/direct. Unit tests (interpret_bazel_test.go + updated contract) + empirical ground-truth back it.

Fixed in PR #88 (squash 649ce7d). Under --suspected, bugbot verify now attaches progress.NewLogRenderer(stdout) (plain log lines, no status.json -> no daemon race) so the slow re-verify pass streams stage/verified/killed lines per finding, plus an upfront announcement. Plain `bugbot verify` keeps the nil sink and byte-identical output. Root cause was the diagnosis of a live aethon run: the pass was working (had promoted 1 of 4 T3->T2, which enqueued a repro agent) but emitted nothing until completion, so it looked dead. Verified via smoke test on a 0-finding store (announcement + summary print; plain verify unchanged); build/vet/golangci-lint/cli-tests green.

Implemented funnel.SimilarFinding (same normalized file + line within DefaultMergeWindow + descTokens/jaccard >= mergeSimilarityThreshold) and a publishAdopt action in cli/publish.go planPublish: an open finding with no published row that matches an already-published open/closing anchor adopts that issue (UpsertPublishedIssue maps the drifted fp -> existing issue number) instead of creating a duplicate. Also absorbs the one-time v1->v2 fingerprint re-key storm. Tested by TestPlanPublish_AdoptsDriftedRediscovery.

store.Fingerprint is now (lens, file, locus); title and raw line removed. funnel.LocusResolver resolves the enclosing top-level symbol via treesitter.Outline, falling back to L:<line> for empty root/unsupported lang/parse fail/package-level lines. Identity is now stable across title rewording and line drift within a symbol. Step-3 exact-fp collisions stage the candidate site; sites deduped at persist and in-memory late-append. SARIF partialFingerprints bumped to bugbotFingerprint/v2. Documented tradeoff: two same-lens findings in one symbol collapse to one multi-site finding; different lenses/files stay distinct. NOTE: v1->v2 is a one-time identity reset (SQL-only migrations cannot recompute symbol fps); bugbot-ky4 absorbs the published-issue storm; v1 suppressions do not carry (re-dismiss once). Cross-file same-pattern merge remains bugbot-wf1. Tests: TestLocusResolver_*, rewritten TestFingerprint_StableAndNormalized, resolver-matched suppression/reverify tests, full suite green (20 pkgs).

Fixed in 703e00d. detectSuiteCmdFor, detectTestCmd, and run_tests bazel narrowing now emit/preserve the canonical argv [bazel,test,--build_tests_only,--test_output=errors,//...]; smokeCmd/detectEcosystem confirmed unaffected. build+vet+tests+lint green for repro/funnel/agent.

Fixed in e974077. prime (recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy/renderPrime) + doctor (checkImageToolchain) no longer claim offline bazel repro is unsupported; they reference bugbot sandbox build + the 3 image ingredients. doctor advisory now conditional (WARN only for non-purpose-built image, INFO otherwise), still advisory-only. cli tests green.

Implemented in 18e66a7. New 'bugbot sandbox build' scaffolds a parameterized offline bazel image (vendor + repo cache baked; warm disk cache committed as a writable layer). Scaffold-only never execs; --run integration-gated. Smoke-tested end-to-end: generates Dockerfile+build.sh, refreshes bugbot.yaml sandbox.image preserving comments/keys. README documents the warm-cache-as-layer rationale. cli tests + lint green.

Bazel tooling now consistent with the repro-stage support (e624e48): deterministic commands use --build_tests_only --test_output=errors (.1), prime/doctor messaging reflects supported offline repro (.2), and the missing offline-image builder ships as 'bugbot sandbox build' (.3). Full gate (build/vet/test/lint across repro,funnel,agent,cli) green; sandbox build smoke-tested. bugbot-1lb (writable disk_cache) left open — partially addressed via warm-cache-as-layer for image-adopting repos.

Fixed in PR #91 (squash-merged to main). realGH.scrubNUL strips NUL from every gh arg at the exec boundary (forkExec rejects NUL with EINVAL), fixing 'fork/exec .../gh: invalid argument' for publish AND review; sanitizeControlChars (byte-wise C0 strip, except tab/newline/CR) cleans the rendered issue body + title. The 'repro not sent' symptom was the crash aborting the whole run before repro-bearing T1/T0 findings could publish (the shown T2 finding correctly had no ReproPath). Tests: TestScrubNUL, TestSanitizeControlChars, TestRenderIssueBody_StripsControlChars, TestApplyPublish_StripsNULFromModelText, TestRenderIssueBody_SanitizeAndTruncateJointly.

Added 'design-sandbox --guide' (outside-in): emits the full designer brief — verdict, candidate sandbox block (YAML), mined repo evidence, the sandbox-proposal JSON schema, the constraint rules, and a convergence loop (edit bugbot.yaml sandbox block, then 'bugbot doctor --verify-sandbox' until PASS) — for the LLM driving bugbot, with no provider required. --guide and --agent are mutually exclusive (cobra MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive); the agent/error branch is gated on !guideMode and a single post-verify 'if guideMode { return printDesignGuide(...) }' covers OK/failed/not-run verdicts. Convergence routes through doctor --verify-sandbox because synthesisSandbox/verifyCandidate ignore the existing block. renderDesignGuide is a pure function; rules/evidence/schema reuse the inside-out single sources of truth (designSandboxSystemPrompt, new shared writeMinedArtifacts with sorted labels, sandboxProposalSchema). The failure hint now mentions both --agent and --guide. Tests: TestRenderDesignGuide (3 verdicts + invariants + sorted evidence), TestRenderDesignGuideNoEvidence, TestDesignGuideMutuallyExclusiveWithAgent, TestDesignGuideEmitsBriefOnVerifySkip. Full ./internal/cli/ suite + go vet green; smoke-tested via the root binary.

Hardened against concurrent-writer corruption with a consistent DB handler + single-writer enforcement + recovery. (1) PREVENTION: store.Open now takes an exclusive cross-process flock on <path>.lock (writers: scan/daemon/review/repro/publish); a second writer refuses with *ErrLocked naming the holder pid. Kernel releases the flock on process death, so a crashed writer leaves no stale lock. OpenReadOnly (report/leads/metrics/export/status + Diagnose reopen) skips the lock so readers coexist with the writer under WAL. (2) CONSISTENT HANDLER: every accessor routes through Store.exec/query/queryRow/queryRows/withTx (internal/store/ops.go) — one place for annotateErr + bounded retry on transient classes (SQLITE_BUSY, IOERR_SHORT_READ checkpoint race); CORRUPT never retried. (3) RECOVERY: Store.Check (PRAGMA quick_check) runs each daemon cycle (skips the cycle on corruption) and in 'bugbot doctor'; store.Recover (backup corrupt->corrupt-<ts>, rebuild fresh schema via migrations, salvage readable rows table-by-table, quick_check, atomic swap) is wired to 'bugbot doctor --repair' (operator-invoked, never automatic; takes the writer lock so it refuses while a writer runs). synchronous stays NORMAL (WAL+NORMAL is corruption-safe; the fix is the writer lock). Tests: lock refuse/release/readonly-coexist/memory-exempt, Check clean, Recover healthy-roundtrip + damaged-source-produces-clean-db, retry classification (constraint NOT transient). go build ./..., go vet, gofmt clean; store/cli/daemon suites pass incl. -race on store.

Restructured finder (finderSystemBase) and shared verifier (verifierToolParagraph) prompts into PRIMARY (LSP nav, read_symbol, outline, find_usages, plus finder-only get_package_context/package_graph/post_lead) vs FALLBACK (grep, whole-file read_file, list_dir). Added fallback framing to the grep and read_file tool Defs so the schema-level signal agrees. Verifier omits finder-only tools (refuter independence) and never names sandbox_exec. New tests TestToolGuidance_PrimaryBeforeFallback and TestToolGuidance_FinderOnlyVsShared pin the hierarchy and the finder-only/shared split; all existing prompt regression tests still pass. Decision: kept status_note Def-only to avoid encouraging note-spam over output turns.

Shipped in PR #94 (squash ceed4dd). Durable cross-lens dedup: lens-independent locus_key (sha256(normFile,locus), migration 015, indexed) + per-candidate point-lookup OpenFindingsByLocusKey in triage process(); on a different-lens hit gated by SimilarFinding it folds as corroboration (AddCorroboratingLenses+AppendFindingSites) instead of forwarding a duplicate primary. Pipelined+idempotent, no DB sweep; fixes the interrupted-run resume gap where in-memory cluster state is not rebuilt from persisted findings. Catches the reader_writer + encoder_tool:64 real dups; graph_transfer 13/14 (line-fallback locus, jaccard 0.176) remains out of scope (threshold case). Oracle APPROVE-WITH-NITS (nits addressed). NOTE: TestInterruptMatrix/B1 is pre-existing flaky on main (interrupt-timing WAL count), unrelated to this change.

Implemented read-only dep-source roots (internal/agent/dep_source.go: DepSourceRoots, NewDepSourceRoots discovers GOROOT/src + GOMODCACHE; per-root traversal-safe resolve). Extended readFileTool with NewReadFileWithDepRoots (extraRoots) so a verifier can read stdlib or third-party source; in-repo path still wins when both exist; /symlink escape rejected from any root. Reworded verifierToolParagraph + verifierRefutationCriteria in internal/funnel/prompt.go so the in-repo read tool is no longer falsely claimed to reach GOROOT/module cache. Unit tests: TestDepSourceRoots_* + TestNewReadFileWithDepRoots_* cover resolve-in-root, /absolute escape rejection, symlink escape rejection, in-repo-still-works, dep-source reads, and escape rejection from the constructor.

Implemented and merged in PR #96 (squash cbc21df). The split arbiter is now an agentic investigator with a DISTINCT budget (StageLimits.ArbiterLimits + BudgetConfig.ArbiterTokenClaim ~5x refuter, default 5M; arbiterRunnerLimits draws from the verify pool at the larger claim; DefaultArbiterMaxIterations=50) — refuter budget (verifyClaim/VerifierLimits) provably untouched (AC1/AC5). Arbiter-only read_file dep-source reach (GOROOT/src + Go module cache via mi5.18 DepSourceRoots, existence-aware after oracle MUST-FIX); refuters stay repo-rooted. arbiterSystemPrompt rewritten to drive-to-ground + MANDATORY verify-the-decisive-claim; REQUIRED structured evidence field on arbiterSchema, surfaced in the verification trace (AC2). Refuter independence preserved, no debate, post_lead absent (AC3). Stats.ArbiterTokens + ArbiterBudgetStops (AC6). Oracle review APPROVE (1 MUST-FIX + 6 nits all fixed). CI green (build/vet/test, golangci-lint, CodeQL). AC4 machinery is in place and pipeline-gated by mi5.19's scripted fixtures; the live-model flip proof on the 7 controller-repo splits is the recorded-corpus step tracked on mi5.19.

Adjudicated all 3 disputed controller splits + the negative control against source (comment 2026-06-24). MIXED outcome: speed_config.cc:29 REAL (WITH_DEFAULT silently defaults missing fields, contradicting the documented throw-on-missing contract — FP label was wrong, arbiter keep correct); remote_provision.sh:17 FALSE POSITIVE as filed (set -e fires in if-bodies, empirically verified — arbiter over-kept on the mi5.20 fabricated+incorrect probe); pwm_pin.cc:21 not a live bug (unreachable null — defensive nit at low sev, arbiter over-kept); wheel_jog_controller.cc:31 negative control HOLDS (uninitialized virtual PanicHandler base => watchdog OnPanic is a silent no-op, real bug). Actions taken: AC1 done (adjudication recorded); AC2 done (mi5.17 AC4 retired, grounding logged as PASS via comment on mi5.17); AC3 done (arbiter over-keep tuning bead bugbot-mi5.22 filed with the two failure analyses); AC4 done (labeled split ground-truth set scoped into mi5.22 design — extend eval cases 7&8 with the four real mechanisms).

AC1-4 shipped in PR #46 (merged fd25ffa). AC5 now satisfied by the scripted eval gate (PR #96, confirmed passing 2026-06-24): TestBenchmarkSuite shows split-arbiter-keeps (real reachable nil-deref, 1-refute split) survives with tp=1 — a real bug surviving a 1-refute split that pre-arbiter majority would demote — while precision stays 1.000 and clean-fp=0 (split-arbiter-refutes + clean-code canaries yield 0 findings). Precision does not drop; the required fixture exists. Recorded-corpus precision tracking is covered by mi5.19.

Closes mi5.7 AC3. Default-on decision MADE and shipped: scan.cartographer defaults true (PR #75, 13905e6/7c08260). The owner's call reframed the metric from raw-token-reduction (wrong lens for an additive agent) to valid-findings-per-token: per-run instrumentation persists cartographer_enabled + verified + input/output tokens into scan_runs.stats_json (sliceable on/off), and 'bugbot metrics' (store.RunMetrics) prints the verified/1k series + pooled on/off comparison. Prerequisite think-strip + budget fix shipped (PR #76, 2148f0c — cartographer was injecting raw <think> reasoning as the summary). Deep-strategy injection decision: only the default sweep-wide finderTask carries the summary block (documented known-limitation on mi5.7; contractTraceDeep/stateTraceDeep unchanged). Ongoing validation = watch the on/off verified/1k trend across real dogfood runs (a continuous series, not a one-shot gate), so it is not a close blocker.

Primary decided deliverable DONE+merged: the self-contained SCRIPTED CI gate (PR #96, cbc21df) — split-arbiter-refutes (clean code, arbiter REFUTES => 0 findings; also a regression gate vs the pre-mi5.17 one-shot arbiter via the REQUIRED evidence field) + split-arbiter-keeps (real reachable nil-deref survives a 1-refute split). Confirmed passing 2026-06-24: TestBenchmarkSuite aggregate precision=1.000, clean-fp=0, split-arbiter-keeps tp=1 (AC1, AC2, AC4). The one-time live capture happened (real MiniMax-M3, transcripts /tmp/arbiter-live-transcripts; build-tagged harness internal/funnel/arbiter_live_test.go), and the recommendation (flip rate + no false demotion — wheel_jog preserved) is recorded on mi5.17. AC3's 'offline ModeRecorded 7-split corpus' is SUPERSEDED by bugbot-mi5.22: the mi5.21 adjudication falsified the original FP labels (speed_config is a real bug, not an FP), so recording the original labels would bake in wrong ground truth; the corrected labeled split ground-truth set is scoped as mi5.22 AC3 (offline eval fixtures for all four mechanisms). Harness is corrected (expectations now match adjudication) and awaits a commit/PR decision.

Fixed: gated all probe/execution prompt language on hasSandbox and added an explicit no-execution prohibition (verifierNoSandboxParagraph) appended to both verifier and arbiter prompts when no exec tool is wired; probe-as-evidence guidance now lives only in the sandbox paragraph. Root-cause fix (the offending text was in the SHARED verifier blocks, so refuters are covered too). AC1 done (prompt gated). AC2 done (TestArbiterPrompt_NoSandbox_ForbidsProbeFabrication + extended StdlibSourceDirective/WithoutSandbox tests; full funnel+eval suites + build/vet/gofmt/golangci-lint all green). AC3 done (bounded live replay of tools/remote_provision.sh through the read-only arbiter: evidence is now all file:line, zero fabricated probes, fabricatedProbeHint detector passes). Residual over-keep (arbiter still keeps via wrong from-memory bash reasoning, no longer via a fake probe) is bugbot-mi5.22, not this defect.

Shipped in PR #98 (squash-merged to main, commit 0a2645b). Phases 1+2 of the tool-health signal: (A) OBJECTIVE/always-on infra-failure capture at the agent runner dispatch seam (agent.WithToolHealthSink + typed *agent.ToolHealthError; sandbox_exec is the first producer, returning it only on genuine runtime-infra failure). (B) SUBJECTIVE/flag-gated report_tool_issue complaint tool (Scan.ToolComplaints / BUGBOT_SCAN_TOOL_COMPLAINTS, off by default) with domain.Severity validated via ParseSeverity. Both converge on funnel.recordToolIssue -> Result.Stats.ToolIssues (deduped) + progress.KindToolUnhealthy, surfaced in status.json (Status.UnhealthyTools), the live pane, and the scan summary. Harness-meta only — never leaks into findings/reports/SARIF. Severity reuses domain.Severity (no new vocabulary). Oracle-reviewed (GO-WITH-NITS); fixes SF1 (sandbox path-escape misclassification), SF2 (ctx-cancellation misclassification), N1 (rune-safe summary truncation), N2 (verifier-aimed caveat) all landed with regression tests. CI green (build/vet/test-race/golangci-lint/CodeQL). NOTE: implemented via a 3-agent fan-out (agent/progress/config packages) + orchestrator integration of funnel/cli; a tooling path-resolution gotcha (subagent vs orchestrator relative paths resolving to different worktrees) was recovered by consolidating into the feature worktree. Phase 3 deferred -> follow-up bead.

Rename tracking implemented as rewrite-in-place (not a path-alias table): renames detected in the daemon's existing lastSeen..head range walk (ingest.Repo.ChangedFiles already runs git diff --name-status -M) trigger store.RenameFindingIdentity, which updates finding file/fingerprint/locus_key and carries the fingerprint forward on suppressions, repro_attempts, and published_issues in one transaction. Idempotent by construction (SELECT WHERE file=oldFile matches zero rows after first pass), so crash-replay of the range is a no-op. Oracle-reviewed GREEN; merged to feature/ezmx-dedup at 2a157e9.

Non-symbol locus fallback now content-anchored: C:<12-hex sha256 of normalized line>#<ordinal> (normalization = trim + collapse whitespace, CRLF-safe; ordinal = 1-based occurrence count among identical normalized lines, disambiguating duplicate lines so anchor collisions cannot fold distinct defects). Identity no longer re-mints on edits above the line. S:<symbol> path byte-for-byte unchanged; blank/unreadable/out-of-range still fall back to L:<line>. Existing L:-anchored suppressions preserved via dual-lookup (IsSuppressed takes a legacyLocusKey; chosen over migration because old L: loci cannot be reliably distinguished post-hoc); findings' own locus keys intentionally not migrated - one-time non-duplicate identity churn on next scan, suppression continuity was the acceptance scope. LLM dedup arbiter landed (internal/funnel/dedup_arbiter.go): one bounded zero-tool RunJSON turn fired ONLY at collision sites the deterministic gates cannot resolve - triage step-5 cluster collisions failing the jaccard gate (clusterJaccardCollision: near + same/unknown kind + jaccard<threshold) and durableCrossLensFold's SimilarFinding miss. Reuses verifier-role client + verify budget so spend is ledgered with no new role. Per-scan cap DefaultDedupArbiterCap=25 (constant by explicit choice), graceful pass-through with DedupArbiterSkippedCap stat. New stats are visibility overlays - DuplicateRate numerator disjointness preserved by construction. Durable adoption at triage landed: durableCrossLensFold widened from exact-locusKey/OPEN-only to same-file line-window (SimilarFinding breadth, new store.FindingsByFileWindow + migration 021 idx_findings_file_line) across open/fixed/dismissed rows, gated on defect_kind compatibility + differing lens. Three triage-terminal outcomes (candidate never reaches verify - no refuter panel spent): OPEN -> corroboration fold (MergedCrossLensDurable), with the ezmx.2 dedup arbiter as fallback on SimilarFinding miss (yes->adopt, else keep); DISMISSED -> candidate fingerprint suppressed via the same store.AddSuppression entry point maintainer dismissals use (DroppedSuppressedDurable; converges bugbot-oiem); FIXED -> store.ReopenAsRegression flips the row back to open in place, same id/fingerprint, tier+repro preserved, swept_at cleared (RegressionReopened). Dismissed/fixed branches are deterministic-only (similar==true required) - permanent/state-changing actions never gate on an LLM verdict. Both new stats excluded from DuplicateRate. Dead store.OpenFindingsByLocusKey deleted with doc cleanup. Publish-side adoption kept as backstop. Oracle GREEN; advisory riders (negative dismissed-path tests, doc tightening) included. Merged to feature/ezmx-dedup at a4d4520.

Code-nav caller/callee root-cause fold landed as triage step 5e (after ezmx.3's widened durable fold, before new-primary creation). Design: the structural signal NOMINATES, never decides - on a kind+one-reference-hop hit (same non-empty defect_kind, kinds logic/other excluded as low-signal catch-alls, Reverify candidates excluded per 5d's invariant) the pair routes through the ezmx.2 dedup arbiter; fold only on typed yes; no/unsure/nil/cap-exhausted keep both. In-run branch folds via handleMember(mergeCodeNav) -> MergedRootCauseCodeNav (in DuplicateRate numerator, disjoint by return-before-fallthrough); durable branch looks up open findings via FindingsByFileWindow at the ref location -> MergedCrossLensDurableCodeNav (excluded). Reference lookups reuse the agent CodeNav/LSP machinery (thin programmatic seam, no duplicate backend), bounded <=1 query per (file,StartLine,symbol) per scan, memoized; nav nil/error degrades to pass-through. Two review rounds: RED (over-merge on kind+hop alone, missing Reverify guard, untested durable branch, cache-key collision, ref-order nondeterminism) fixed via arbiter gating + guards + tests; GREEN round 2 with rider tests (empty-kind exclusion call-count, caller-first kind-bucket isolation). Original implementor aborted mid-fix; finisher inherited worktree and completed. Residual note: durable branch winner selection is LSP-ref-order dependent when multiple distinct persisted same-kind findings all clear the arbiter for one candidate - pathological, documented here for future tightening. Merged to feature/ezmx-dedup at 4131594.

Backlog reconcile landed: periodic daemon drain on its own ReconcileInterval timer (default 24h; config yaml + BUGBOT_DAEMON_RECONCILE_INTERVAL env + cli wired, Validate>0). Groups OPEN findings by file, deterministically nominates pairs (kind gate first via sameOrUnknownKind, then SimilarFinding closeness; oldest-CreatedAt-first with ID tie-break, one pair per finding per cycle - converges over cycles), confirms each nomination via the ezmx.2 dedup arbiter seam (typed yes only; per-cycle cap independent of per-scan cap; verifier-role ledgered), merges newer into older-canonical via AppendFindingSites+AddCorroboratingLenses, closes the duplicate StatusSuperseded with typed Finding.SupersededBy canonical-fingerprint pointer + prose SupersededReason (migration 022; ValidateFindingState clause h forbids provenance without the status; UpsertFinding clears provenance on genuine same-fingerprint re-mint). Gated on storeHealthy BEFORE any write (explicitly not copying the bugbot-lcr0 verify-drain gap) and the day budget (skip reschedules). Idempotent: superseded rows leave the next OPEN query. planPublish now closes superseded findings' GitHub issues like fixed/dismissed with a canonical-fingerprint close comment; RenameFindingIdentity rewrites superseded_by as its 5th column. Two review rounds: RED (dangling provenance on regression re-mint; publish surface orphaned duplicate issues) then GREEN. Merged to feature/ezmx-dedup at 3c3aac9.
